commands.review_summary:
The branch removes explicit false defaults from the click command's `--no-scroll` and `--no-bubbles` Commander options and adds a parser-level regression test for default and opt-out behavior.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main sets both negated Commander options to `false` by default and then treats `false` as disabled. The PR's parser-level test is a focused reproduction path for the real CLI parsing gap that the older mocked command test missed.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is contributor or maintainer-provided real behavior proof for the live CLI/browser path.

target.title: fix(click): restore scroll + bubbling defaults (--no-scroll/--no-bubbles were inverted and dead)
target.body:
## Problem

`sweetlink click` is documented to scroll the element into view and dispatch a bubbling click by default (see `tests/click.test.ts` "includes scroll and bubbling by default"), but on the real CLI it does neither, and the `--no-scroll` / `--no-bubbles` flags have no effect at all.

## Root cause

Both options pass a third argument of `false`:

```ts
.option("--no-scroll", "Skip scrolling the element into view before clicking", false)
.option("--no-bubbles", "Dispatch the click event without bubbling", false)
```

For a `--no-x` flag Commander defines an option named `x` whose default is `true` and which becomes `false` when the flag is present. Passing an explicit default of `false` overrides that implicit `true`, so `options.scroll` and `options.bubbles` are `false` even when the flags are absent. The action then computes:

```ts
scrollIntoView: options.scroll !== false,  // false !== false -> false
bubbles: options.bubbles !== false,        // false !== false -> false
```

so clicks never scroll into view and never bubble, and passing `--no-scroll` / `--no-bubbles` changes nothing (the value is already `false`). The `--no-devtools` option in `src/index.ts` is written the correct way (no default), which is the in-repo precedent.

## Why the existing test didn't catch it

`tests/commands/click-command.test.ts` mocks `readCommandOptions` to return `{ selector, timeout }`, with no `scroll`/`bubbles` keys. That makes `options.scroll` `undefined`, so `undefined !== false` is `true` and the assertion `scrollIntoView: true` passes — but only because real Commander parsing is bypassed. In production `readCommandOptions` returns `optsWithGlobals()`, where the bogus default puts `scroll: false` / `bubbles: false`.

## Fix

Drop the `false` default from both options so the `--no-` flags behave normally (default on, flag turns them off).

## Tests

Added `tests/commands/click-defaults.test.ts`, which drives the real registered command through `program.parseAsync` (mocking only the downstream side effects, leaving `readCommandOptions` real). It asserts scroll + bubbling are on with no flags, and that `--no-scroll` / `--no-bubbles` each turn off exactly one. These fail on the current code (both default to `false`) and pass after the fix.

The rest of the suite is green; the only other failures, `tests/token-cache.test.ts`, are pre-existing and environment-dependent (they need `SWEETLINK_SECRET`) and fail the same way on an unmodified checkout. `oxfmt --check` and `oxlint --deny-warnings` are clean on the changed files.

Found and fixed with the help of the Claude CLI.
target.author: devyrpauli
